fields.name: probe
fields.label: Probe (content capture)
fields.description: What the crawl captures per file beyond stat metadata: 'off' = paths/sizes only (fastest), 'hash' = SHA-256 content identity (fills the catalogue), 'full' = hash + fmeta metadata (format, mime, tabular stats). The real-world campaign default.

fields.name: path
fields.label: Path override (crawl this directory instead)
fields.description: Absolute directory to crawl IN PLACE of the synthetic demo NAS. When set, the prepare step seeds nothing and the campaign walks this tree where it lives (root = parent dir, prefix = basename/). Leave empty for the self-contained demo (seeds 63 files under /tmp/mekhan-campaign-demo). Point it at a real corpus — an NFS mount, a workstation home — and raise batch_size/max_batches to match.

fields.name: resume_from
fields.label: Resume after path (cursor from a failed run)
fields.description: Manual-retry knob: seed the campaign cursor so a NEW run resumes after this path instead of walking from the start. Take the value from the failed instance's campaign state (`cursor` accumulator — visible in the instance marking / `mekhan logs <id>`), or leave empty to re-walk everything (safe either way: fold upserts are idempotent on (file_server_id, path), a from-scratch re-run only costs time). The path must still exist — a vanished cursor is a hard error by design.

fields.name: batch_size
fields.label: Batch size (files per fold batch)
fields.description: Files per durable fold-batch publish. Demo default 10; for a real corpus use 1000-5000 (probe=full needs ≤ ~500 — each item carries its fmeta blob inside one NATS publish).

fields.name: max_batches
fields.label: Max batches per iteration
fields.description: Filled batches per loop iteration (one executor job crawls batch_size × max_batches files, then the cursor loops). Demo default 2; for a real corpus use 10-25 so each chunk is tens of thousands of files.

data.label: Prepare target (seed demo NAS, or pass override through)
data.description: The single place the demo-vs-real conditional lives. With Start's `path` empty: writes a deterministic 63-file tree into /tmp/mekhan-campaign-demo (cleaning any prior run first; 63 is deliberately NOT divisible by the demo's 20-files-per-chunk so the final iteration exercises the trailing-partial-batch path: 20+20+20+3) and emits root=/tmp + prefix=mekhan-campaign-demo/. With `path` set: seeds NOTHING and emits root=<parent>/prefix=<basename>/ so the campaign walks the given tree in place. The crawl step borrows `{{ seed.root }}`/`{{ seed.prefix }}` — downstream placeholders stay dumb.

data.label: Crawl campaign
data.description: Cursor loop: each iteration crawls ONE bounded chunk (max_batches × batch_size files), resuming after the previous iteration's cursor. The accumulators are the ONLY state crossing iterations: `cursor` folds `crawl.last_path`, `total` sums `crawl.count`. Engine work per iteration is constant no matter how big the corpus is. ACCOUNTING PATTERN: the loop's mergeExpr folds on the CONTINUE transition only — the exit path skips it — so the condition keeps looping until a chunk comes back EMPTY (`exhausted && count == 0`). The merge-skipped exit chunk then contributes zero and `campaign.total` is exact; the price is one extra readdir-cheap iteration that walks nothing.

data.label: Crawl one chunk → fold sink
data.description: The file-ops `crawl` op in SINK MODE (docs/32 batch-fold): walks `{{ seed.root }}`/`{{ seed.prefix }}` (the prepare step's resolved target — synthetic NAS or path override) resuming after `{{ campaign.cursor }}` (a Loop-accumulator borrow interpolated into the config — empty string on iteration 0 means from-the-start), stops after `{{ start.max_batches }}` filled batches of `{{ start.batch_size }}` files (NUMBER-typed borrows: full-value substitution is typed, so placeholders aren't string-only), and PUBLISHES each batch durably to the `INVENTORY_FOLD` JetStream stream instead of emitting channel items (note: no channels declared on this node). The publish-ack lands BEFORE the cursor advances, and mekhan's fold consumer upserts rows idempotently on (file_server_id, path) — so a retried or re-run chunk never duplicates. `sink.file_server_id` is itself a borrow (`{{ start.file_server }}`), as is `probe` (`{{ start.probe }}`): with probing on, every file is read once during the walk — SHA-256 content identity (and, on 'full', the fmeta metadata blob) rides each fold item, so the catalogue fills in the same pass (hash → content-addressed entry, blob → file_metadata/mime). Probe failures never kill a chunk; they're counted per chunk in `probe_errors` and totalled by the campaign. Outputs are slim scalars; `exhausted` is the loop's exit signal and `last_path` the next iteration's cursor.
